title: PathwayBench: Benchmarking Pathway Activity Scoring Methods for Pseudobulk Single-Cell Transcriptomics
description: PathwayBench is a comprehensive benchmarking framework that systematically evaluates five pathway activity scoring methods (ssGSEA, GSVA, Z-score, AUCell, UCell) for pseudobulk data derived from single-cell and single-nucleus RNA sequencing. The benchmark assesses six robustness criteria -- biological relevance, aggregation stability, low-abundance gene sensitivity, outlier robustness, normalization stability, and sample-size stability -- across ten disease datasets from CellxGene Discover, with literature-backed ground truth pathways from Reactome. Includes a Snakemake pipeline for full reproducibility and an interactive Shiny app (PathwayBench Advisor) for method recommendation.
notes:
Data files for Zenodo deposition:
1. data/pathways/ - Gene set GMT files
2. data/pseudobulk/ - Pseudobulk expression matrices (RDS)
3. results/scores/ - Pathway activity scores per method
4. results/evaluation/ - Criteria evaluation results (CSV + RDS)
5. results/figures/ - Publication figures (PDF)
